quotes.text: You keep calling it a negotiation. I call it a countdown.
quotes.speaker: Die Hard (1988)
quotes.context: McClane taunting Hans Gruber over the radio
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — McClane's wisecracks at Gruber are exactly this clipped and combative, so it fits the banter perfectly. The subtle giveaway is that 'countdown' is slightly too tidy a mic-drop; his real zingers tend to be rougher and more self-deprecating.

quotes.speaker: Rocky (1976)
quotes.context: Rocky Balboa addressing Adrian; "Yo, Adrian! It's me, Rocky." The phrase appears in the 1976 film.

quotes.text: Check the motion tracker again. If it reads clear, it's lying.
quotes.speaker: Aliens (1986)
quotes.context: A marine, mid-sweep of the colony
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — it has the clipped, tense cadence of the colony sweep, but the real film never spells the dread out this neatly.

quotes.text: I didn't steal it. I just never gave it back.
quotes.speaker: Catch Me If You Can (2002)
quotes.context: Frank Abagnale Jr. explaining one of his early cons to an FBI agent
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Frank's cheerful, slippery logic in self-justification is exactly the film's comic engine, and DiCaprio delivers lines with this kind of breezy semantic dodge throughout. The giveaway is it's almost too quotable; the film's best Frank lines tend to be embedded in longer, charming riffs.

quotes.text: The best way to spread Christmas cheer is singing loud for all to hear.
quotes.speaker: Elf (2003)
quotes.context: Buddy the Elf sharing his philosophy on holiday spirit
